fault_hypothesis: Lines 69-70 incorrectly remove the last element when both streams share the same final value, but this is wrong behavior — duplicates should be preserved. The entire if-block at line 69 is the bug.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the pop should never be true for valid merged output — the pop is always wrong.
After initialization, merged must be empty.
The then-branch of the faulty if should never be executed — popping the last element is always incorrect.
The merged result must contain exactly as many elements as the sum of both inputs.
